reviews.review_title: Blocked Models and No Smudge Tool
reviews.review_description: I was amazed at the models that they provided. However, they are blocked which leads to me not drawing true anatomy. And there’s no smudging tool you have to use the eraser to lightly shade. I’m an artist myself 2D and 3D art, I must be spoiled or it just feels like it has more potential.￼

reviews.review_title: Amazing app for ALL artistic levels!
reviews.review_description: I purchased Gesture VR approximately 2 years ago and highly recommend this app whether you’re a beginner who is new to life drawing or an experienced artist who wants to brush up on their skills (or keep them toned!). There’s a great variety of poses to choose from, and if any become familiar, you can fully rotate them to give yourself a fresh challenge. Drawing via passthrough mode using traditional materials is a near perfect experience under the correct room lighting conditions. I hope the developer will continue to support and update this app in the future… perhaps add new models/poses (or even full colour??? I would happily pay for a version 2.
This would be a terrific app. If it were easier. To use. I spent hours. Trying to open. The left menu. To no avail.

reviews.review_title: Plen air vr painting in gesture vr?
reviews.review_description: Its good software. I am an artist who paints real paints, i have been looking for a software where i could paint like plen air painting in vr, so only need to make your real canvas visible, so it is almost possible with gesture vr IF you could add some real plases in to the software. I hope you will see this review. It is so close now.please. please.
Difficult to operate, not intuitive. I want my money back
Would be perfect with hand tracking

reviews.review_title: Would give this 5 but for 1 serious flaw
reviews.review_description:
I bought this app to compliment my Logitec MX Ink pen and it works great with it. I actually think for sketching that Gesture VR is a really good app. 
HOWEVER...... there is a serious flaw in the app which basically renders the app in my opinion useless!! I don't like to give a bad rating but I have to reiterate what some other users have pointed out (several months ago) that after so long sketching on a sheet, you find that you suddenly can't draw or edit the sheet anymore. You can add another sheet to your board and draw/edit away again but when you go back to the sheet you were sketching on it doesn't allow you to draw anymore (even when you reload the app).
This is incredibly frustrating as on 3 occasions when I have been drawing a portrait for a fair length of time I suddenly can't sketch any more. I'm amazed the devs haven't fixed this or even answered the previous reviews which point out the same problem. 
Therefore, until I hear of a patch that fixes this, I simply won't be using it because what is the point because you can't finish any drawings!!!
I basically feel that I've wasted my money (unless it's fixed of course).
I therefore could not recommend this app to anyone at the moment because even though it is very good you cannot finish any drawings rendering the app in my opinion a waste of effort and time. 
I will of course change my review and give it 5 stars if the Devs can please, please - please fix it. Thanks!

reviews.review_title: Best drawing stability I have seen yet
reviews.review_description:
The stability is much better with a logitech mx ink pen. Sketching large is cool. Tapered lines that respond to imaginary pen pressure are gorgeous.Its a really great tool to study art without an expensive art school. Works great on my quest 2.

The biggest downside for me is the dependence on opacity to create value. All vr art programs use it, but this program depends on it. I wish opacity was replaced with particle effects, or the addition of a blending stump.

I really like this app but at times the pen won’t touch the canvas when I’m getting to by the edge of it. I also find it hard to work out exactly when the pen will start drawing, how close to have it.
also it would be nice to be able to lock down the model as I press the wrong button and it moves/the lights move etc 
I am going to still keep trying as I like the idea but I feel it could be a bit more precise

I’ve been a professional model for more than 30 years, and I’m so glad I stumbled on this because it’s actually hard for me to draw from other models in person. I tend to get distracted by how awesome they are—unless they’re not, in which case I want throw them off the stand and get up there myself. You see the problem. So it’s cool to draw from scans of real, great models. ￼￼ And I find it absolutely hilarious that there is a heater on the stand, I’m glad the virtual models won’t get cold.

I have suggestions. I would love more control of the eraser, to be able to use it as a subtractive tool. Control of the size, control of the shape. ￼

I have not found a way to lock the pose and the lighting in freeform. You could call it the “tape” feature, after a model calling out they need help marking the pose so they can take a break!

No reclining poses except from the statues?

A more detailed skull would be awesome. 

I am using a Meta 2 with a Logitech stylus.

I have the quest 2 and the mixed reality does not work great in that headset, this is why I had doubts about this app at first. I have being using it for some time now and the truth is that I do not miss that feature at all. I guess it is great to have if you have the quest 3, but the in app drawing works great and it is entertaining and very useful to practice anatomy.

I loved the game it would be 4-5 stars. But I keep running into one problem, when drawing and Im doing very simple shading it glitches and I am no longer able to draw on the sketchbook even when I exit game, restart the headset, turn it off, un download, redownload you name it. This is incredibly annoying to not be able to finish an art piece and so far there is no solution on why it happens or how to fix it.

(Pro Tip: If you have a 3rd party headstrap, pop out your Quest's face guard and tilt your headset up a bit so you can use a real sketchbook more easily!)

This is a solid, lovely experience! Thank you so much for developing this.

As suggested by others, I'd love an option to tilt the models in any direction! Functionality-wise, my recommendation would be to have a toggle that allows you to use the trigger to rotate it much like how we currently rotate the light source. 
Alternatively, you could have a section in the menu dedicated to the model you're currently viewing, like a little tiny version that you could grab and rotate, and that would reflect its position in the studio. Just a thought :-)

It'd also be nice to have the option to "project" grids and lines onto the models!

But yeah, I love this a lot and plan to use it for many more hours :-)

reviews.review_title: The only creative app I’ve enjoyed!
reviews.review_description: I had fun with the mix reality mode works very well you can more around your sculpture very intuitively. You can adjust the brush grip and art board size was very easy. The built in tutorials were useful and easy to follow/understand for beginners

reviews.review_title: Amazing art experience that needs work.
reviews.review_description:
I love everything about this app except for what matters most... the way the pencil draws. I took a blank canvas and tried to write my signature using no stabilization and then various levels of stabilization. There was nothing smooth and responsive (or realistic) about it.

I then jumped into Painting VR and was blown away (all over again) by how smooth, responsive, and realistic those tools are. I hope that Gestures VR can figure out how to improve the clunky, inferior drawing experience so I can draw even  better flowing effortless gestures.

reviews.review_title: Superb! But please add two features
reviews.review_description:
1. Custom obj (created in C4D, Blender or whatever)
2. Import reference images so that we can learn to draw from jpg, png
Don’t bother purchasing for Quest 2, the visuals are awful. Never purchasing from the developer again.

developer_response.body: Sorry you feel that way. Apart from the black-and-white passthrough on Quest 2, the visuals are largely the same across all devices, and we believe they are well-represented in our screenshots and trailers.

reviews.review_description: Great app. It’s apps like this that remind us that VR gives you opportunities you wouldn’t otherwise have.  Just a suggestion for us left handed artists — please move the haptic feedback to the left hand when in left hand mode :) feels funny to touch canvas with left hand and feel the jolt in right hand.  Small bug there but otherwise wonderful app. Well done!

[edit] I lowered my score from 5 to 3 because the program has changed. The number and quality of the models has decreased. The software I bought was amazing and while some of the technical parts have improved- there’s no denying the attractiveness of the models has been very harshly degreased. And they have underwear on that in many instances is very distracting to the gesture line. (On the other side of that though- the hair has been put in caps so that is actually better so… that’s a positive change, it’s just not enough to fully counter the underwear),I can’t say this is as good as the product I bought- enough to lower the score by 2 stars. My hope is we will see this get a full release and then the licenses that were lost due to… whatever- are released and we get the high quality “run to class because you can’t wait to draw” models back.

This is like being back in art school! The control over the lights is what makes it just a little bit better. If you’ve ever tried working from 2D shots and found them lacking… a lot- this is a dream come true. The scans are incredible and I’m on a Q2! This is a must for anybody who loves figure drawing and gesture exercises. It’s so easy to recommend. All o can say is bring us more! More studios, more models and more poses! This is just wonderful and I could spend hours working in Gesture VR!

It captures the gesture drawing perfectly, as others have commented, it would be great to increase the model catalogue or let import your own models. I can't be compared with other art apps, like Vermillion, this is something different and unique. Also more options like changing the model scale or lighting would be a great improvement, maybe it's possible, I have only been 30 min making 5 or 6 sketches. I like it a lot.

The dream for VR is to allow you to do things that would otherwise be difficult to do in real life and do those things as well as or better. This is really close.

I usually don’t have the time to get to a real life drawing class, or do sessions practicing gesture in museums or cafes, but with this app I can study at my leisure, practice, and even better not feel self conscious about what I’m doing.

The models look fantastic, with smooth shading and the ability to adjust the lighting. Pass through mode is the icing on the cake - I can’t believe I can sketch in my room with a static model in the middle of it, and I can reach for drink without even thinking about taking my headset off.

Sublime.

I had high hopes for the app, seeing as it's from the hands of Nick Ladd who is always a guarantee for high quality. I'm happy to say the app fully lives up to those hopes! It runs surprisingly well on Quest 2 in standalone mode, and it manages to capture a good part of the magic of a real life drawing session.
The drawing feeling is very good and everything you need is in there

l-i-n-k-t-r*e-e/e-d-i-g-s

(Remove - and add a point instead of a * to save some money. Check it out if you're interested.)

The closest thing to live figure drawing in VR. Great art app.

EDIT: after spending more time with Gesture VR, I must say it's now probably my favourite art app.

I do have a few suggestions though: 

- It would be great if we could simply grab models with controllers and freely move them around. Right now it's a bit difficult to have them where you want, especially in MR mode (that is otherwise excellent!) where correct placement in room is even more important.

- Some models are more "blocky" (they almost look like being constructed from voxels), while some are "smooth". I definitely prefer smooth ones.

- Do you plan some Quest3 graphics upgrade? I guess Q3 could handle even higher poly models.

GesetureVR is incredible! Aesthetically 10/10! The overall user experience is great!

The models are high quality, and you have all the tools you'd need and more to practice figure drawing. The app has some great environments that take you back to art school or allow you completely focus on the model in front of you. 

I see this application being very helpful for all artists to hone their skills, but also it gives everyone a chance to try to improve their ability to draw. 

I could spend hours here working on a piece, and the fact that I can invite my friends to the studio is awesome! 

Overall I can't recommend this enough!

reviews.review_title: I really like it with a view caveats
reviews.review_description: I am an artist and am always looking for new tech to try.  Having a model right there like in figure drawing class is awesome! Unfortunately I don’t own the Quest 3 so the pass through is not really something I can use. It’s unfortunate because drawing on paper is much easier than in the app. The pencil precision is almost there but not quite. I think a great workaround would be to have a smudge tool. That way you can get softer gradients on the “paper” . That is the way the majority of artists draw anyways. If you could make this one thing happen I think your app will be much improved. It really has a ton of potential!

Just beginning. Open onto tutorial options, controls and drawing. My long arms barely reach leaning forward the options. No laser trigger pointer? So far no idea how to bring up menu, just how to use it or how to access paper. Light on training but expect better later into hunt and peck discover.

reviews.review_title: Pretty cool; hopefully it can improve
reviews.review_description:
Really great way to get some much needed gesture practice without leaving the house. My two cents on potential improvements/new features:

- Hopefully they can find a way to improve the fidelity of strokes. I feel I'm able to draw more precisely in Vermillion and Painting VR with similar arm movements. The lazybrush might work for some, but I hate the delay.
- Needs a few more lighting options. Shadows are a little too intense no matter how I adjust.
- Would love to be able to see the timer without opening the menu.
- Would love to have a simple stick in my left "hand" to do quick measurements and comparisons. Maybe using the left thumbstick to adjust the stick length, equivalent to moving the thumb up and down on a real stick.

I've been following the development of this app for a long time and seeing it finally get published is amazing. 
My favorite feature is the pass-through function where you can draw on real paper while seeing the model in VR. It's really like being back in the live model studio, with the added functionality of being able to rotate the model and adjust the lighting to right where you want it (while being at home)! It runs great too

All in all I love this app and hope they can get funded to add more models in!

This is now my favorite VR app. I normally do digital sculpting on my phone and have gotten away from figure drawing but this app gives me the opportunity to do figure drawing again. It takes a minute to get used to the pressure of the pencil but once there it feels natural. I wish I could change my camera height as well as change the scale of the model. I could do this for hours, oh wait, I just did. I'll admit, I'm very out of practice drawing in 2D but this should help. Thank you to whoever came up with this. As for the AR, I have a quest 2 and the AR sucks. If I had a quest 3 it would probably be a nice extra feature. 5 stars regardless!

reviews.review_title: Great Drawing App With Few Drawbacks
reviews.review_description:
I understand this app is used for practicing postures and anatomies but I use it to paint sometimesband it works great but the game doesn't fully support this feature in three main ways.

1. There's no color copy tool so you either gotta keep you color palette limited or difficultly try to remix the same color.

2. No layers, this makes sense not to have cause we're sketching on 'paper' but it could still be useful to have

3. There is limited drawing capacity. For example, I'll try to make this super colorful and vibrant painting but halfway through I can't add anymore draw lines. I assume this is cause the app only expects you to do quick sketches mostly

Another thing in general, not being able to upload drawings straight to the Meta Cloud from your headset kinda suck for people who don't have PC's  connect to.

Hope to maybe draw a comic someday and got this to practice. The wonderful music and models combined with the fun studio environment help me get lost in the drawing. It also staves away the anxiety, I really adore this app. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: How can I import some external images?
reviews.review_description: First of all, I have to tell you that love this app and I love the way we can draw pretty similar as the real  world.  I would like to draw with my own reference images because the platform is very cool and feels good to draw in the app.  There are other apps with import feature but this app is perfect for better drawing and I feel that feature is mising.  I hope you consider my sugestion and keep rocking.  Thanks

This is fantastic. 

I've never had the opportunity to have live figure drawing classes in school due to my computer/highly technical focused career. Even the few live drawing classes I took couldn't afford models. We just drew each other and random objects few times. To top that off I come from a semi isolated area where there's no figure drawing groups within a drivable distance and people look at you funny if you try to sneak in a sketch of them.

I'm hoping for additional models and body types for us to study off of.

Hand tracking would also be a cool future update, but not necessary.

I had been waiting for a long time for the opportunity to do figure drawing in VR and I never expected the first attempt to be THAT polished! Amazing job! Excellent brush engine, amazing 3D models, super comfortable experience. I haven't tried the "multiplayer" mode, but boy oh boy, I'm sure I'll love it!

PS. There seems to have a been a slight mishap where the model/pose library got much smaller over the past few months, but to be honest even the smallish current selection is enough poses to enjoy for a long time, and the developers have promised to re-introduce more models/poses over the next few months - I like the idea of having a limited selection; makes you appreciate and focus more on each model before getting the next one. Like music albums or video games before the internet, you get to properly experience it, before moving to the next one.

I've had the app on my radar for a while now, but I've been skeptical of apps like this ever since I attempted to use a mixed reality piano app that hardly worked at all. But this app restored my faith. The pen has a good amount of customization, there are plenty of models, the pen is dynamic, you can customize the direction the light comes from, you can rotate the models, my headset doesn't die while its plugged in, and to top it off, there's multiplayer. I can't think of many things that could make it better. I can't think of anything negative about my experience, I highly recommend it for anyone of any level, there are even guided lessons. All around great app.

Gesture VR is a total dream. I've been waiting for a software like this come out for years, and I'm not let down. 

First off, the functionality of being able to live draw whenever I want is fantastic. No more unlucky fidgety models, no unintentional rough angles, you can set the challenge as hard as you may like. Even the atmosphere is lovely and calming and just really inspires me to draw. 

They say life drawing is one of the quickest way to improve, and they weren't lying. But now, I can do it from the comfort of my couch (which is a blessing in the cold winter months). 

Secondly, the customisation is incredible, such a wide array of models, settings, angles, and lighting to choose from. 

While there is a minor learning curve, it only took about 30 minutes or so until I was drawing fairly close to how I normally do. Not to mention the pass through feature is so much fun! A great way to get started if you're still finding your VR groove. 

Thirdly, the software itself is extremely intuitive, runs so smoothly, and has neat little features (like the lazy drawing tool to help with curves). There was a lot of careful decision making and it shows in how polished it runs.  

Speaking as a professional from the video game industry, this is a must have for any aspiring artists to develop your eye, spacial awareness and drawing skills.

This is more than a game to me .with a lot of art evening classes to expensive  .or shut.  this is a great relief.  Thanks  brilliant 👏 ten stars 🌟 it would be nice to draw with other people. Which don't seem to work at this moment

Using this on Quest 3. I am blown away by how great this app recreates the experience of sitting in a studio and doing figurative drawing. I used to attend weekly sessions some years ago and this app makes me feel like I am back in the mindframe as soon as I splip into it. Love the fact you can turn on passthrough and draw the VR model in "real life" too. Can't recommend this enough if you are wanting to practice your drawing skills! Bravo to the folks who put this app together! I love it!

This app is absolutely amazing!! It has everything you could possibly need, lessons, paper, models to draw and a nice pass though mode which helps me with motion sickness. I’m having a wonderful time and looking forward to getting back into art!
